note: Field Group mutates only display config (form/view display third-party settings) via core Field UI. Cases span three difficulty tiers: `recipe` (easy, response-graded facts about the FieldGroupFormatter plugin type, storage, format types, hooks); `introspection` (medium — a known group is installed on the live Article form display and the agent must read it back via drush); and `execution` (hard — the agent must actually build a group on a form/view display, verified against live site state).

evals.prompt: On this Drupal site (drush available), improve the Article node edit form by grouping fields. Using Field Group, add a collapsible `details` group with machine name `group_eval_meta` and label `Metadata` to the Article default form display, and place the Body field inside that group. Apply it to the live site so the group really shows on the form; don't just describe it. One-line summary.
evals.expected_output: The node.article.default entity_form_display gains a field_group third-party setting `group_eval_meta` (format_type details, label Metadata) whose children include `body`, applied to the live site so the collapsible Metadata group renders on the Article edit form. Verified against live site state.

evals.prompt: On this Drupal site (drush available), organize the Article node's DEFAULT VIEW display (entity_view_display, the rendered node page — NOT the edit form) using Field Group tabs. Create a `tabs` container group with machine name `group_eval_vtabs` (label `Article Tabs`) and, inside it, a `tab` group with machine name `group_eval_vtab` (label `Body Tab`, parent_name `group_eval_vtabs`) that wraps the Body field. Apply it to the live site so the tab really renders on the node view; don't just describe it. One-line summary.
evals.expected_output: The node.article.default entity_VIEW_display gains two field_group third-party settings: `group_eval_vtabs` (format_type tabs) and `group_eval_vtab` (format_type tab, parent_name group_eval_vtabs) whose children include `body`, applied to the live site so the Body tab renders on the Article view. Verified against live site state.

evals.prompt: On this Drupal site (drush available), group fields on the Article node's DEFAULT FORM display using Field Group. Create a `fieldset` group with machine name `group_eval_fieldset` (label `Article Details`) on the Article default form display and place at least two fields inside it (for example Body and the Sections field). Apply it to the live site so the fieldset really shows on the edit form; don't just describe it. One-line summary.
evals.expected_output: The node.article.default entity_form_display gains a field_group third-party setting `group_eval_fieldset` (format_type fieldset, label Article Details) whose children include at least two fields, applied to the live site so the fieldset renders on the Article edit form. Verified against live site state.

evals.prompt: This Drupal site (drush available) already has a Field Group configured on the Article node's default edit form. Inspect the live site and tell me: what is that field group's human-readable label, and what group format type does it use?
evals.expected_output: The Article default form display has a field_group group `group_eval_publish` with label "Publishing Options" and format_type `fieldset` (read from node.article.default entity_form_display third_party_settings.field_group).

evals.prompt: This Drupal site (drush available) has a Field Group named `group_eval_publish` on the Article node's default edit form display. Inspect the live site and list which fields are placed inside that group (its children).
evals.expected_output: The `group_eval_publish` group's `children` array on node.article.default entity_form_display is [body, field_eval_sections] — those two fields are inside the group.

evals.prompt: This Drupal site (drush available) has exactly one Field Group on the Article node's default edit form display. Inspect the live site and tell me the group's machine name and its format_type.
evals.expected_output: The group's machine name is `group_eval_publish` and its format_type is `fieldset` (stored under node.article.default entity_form_display third_party_settings.field_group.group_eval_publish).

evals.prompt: In a Drupal 11 module I want to define a brand-new field_group format (a custom way to render a group of fields). What plugin type do I create, which base class do I extend, and in what namespace/directory does the plugin class live? Name them.
evals.expected_output: Create a FieldGroupFormatter plugin: class in Drupal\<module>\Plugin\field_group\FieldGroupFormatter, annotated @FieldGroupFormatter, extending Drupal\field_group\FieldGroupFormatterBase (interface FieldGroupFormatterInterface).

evals.prompt: On a Drupal site, where/how does field_group store the groups a site builder creates on Manage form display / Manage display? Is there a separate config or content entity per group? Explain so I know how to export them.
evals.expected_output: Groups are stored as third-party settings on the entity form/view display config entity (e.g. core.entity_form_display.<entity>.<bundle>.<mode>.third_party.field_group.<group_name>), not a separate entity, so they export/deploy with drush config:export like any other config.

evals.prompt: A site builder asks which built-in group format types field_group ships for organizing fields on a form. List the core formats (with their machine ids) available in the Add group dropdown.
evals.expected_output: Built-in FieldGroupFormatter formats: Fieldset (fieldset), Details (details), Details sidebar (details_sidebar), Tab (tab), Tabs (tabs), HTML element (html_element); Accordion (accordion/accordion_item) comes from the deprecated field_group_accordion submodule.

evals.prompt: I'm hand-writing a field_group group into an exported core.entity_form_display YAML config. Under the third_party.field_group.<group> key, which keys describe the group's contents, its format, and its nesting under a parent group?
evals.expected_output: The group defines children (fields/subgroups in it), label, parent_name (name of parent group for nesting), region, weight, format_type, and format_settings (keyed by the format's own schema).

evals.prompt: On a Drupal site I want to attach custom classes / JS (#attached, drupalSettings) to one field_group group as it renders on the entity view, without subclassing a formatter. Which field_group hook do I implement?
evals.expected_output: Implement hook_field_group_pre_render() (or hook_field_group_pre_render_alter() to tweak after build) — receives &$element, &$group, &$rendering_object; use it to set #attached/drupalSettings, attributes, classes, #weight on the group.

evals.prompt: I need to add conditional #states visibility logic to a whole field_group group on an entity form (not the front-end view). Which field_group hook(s) fire during form processing that I should use?
evals.expected_output: Use the form-processing hooks: hook_field_group_form_process() / hook_field_group_form_process_alter() (per-group, add #states), and hook_field_group_form_process_build_alter() for cross-group form logic after all groups are processed.

evals.prompt: I want to override the Twig template for field_group's HTML element format in my theme, and target one specific group by name. What is the base template name and how does field_group give me a per-group theme suggestion?
evals.expected_output: Override field-group-html-element.html.twig; field_group_theme_suggestions_alter() adds a per-group suggestion so you can create field-group-html-element--<group-name>.html.twig. (Details/Fieldset/Vertical tabs reuse core templates.)
